steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank follows ‘is well documented’ (a complete independent clause) and precedes ‘sharing nutrients via fungal hyphae and the host tree’s roots, the organisms form a kind of social system’ (a participial phrase followed by another independent clause). The blank must separate the first independent clause from this elaborating structure.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'documented': no punctuation at all. The participial phrase ‘sharing nutrients...’ would then run directly into the first clause, creating an unpunctuated run-on. ✗
  • Choice B — 'documented:': a colon typically introduces a list, a quotation, or a direct restatement. What follows is a participial phrase, not a list or restatement, so a colon is too formal and does not fit the structure here. ✗
  • Choice C — 'documented;': a semicolon marks a strong boundary after the first independent clause. It correctly separates the main assertion from the substantive elaboration that follows, keeping the two parts tightly related. ✓
  • Choice D — 'documented,': a comma alone is too weak to separate an independent clause from the long participial-plus-independent structure that follows — it blurs the boundary and creates a comma splice with the second independent clause. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
